---
title: "hz-cat-cluster-analysis-IA"
format: html
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# for k means clustering
library(factoextra)
library(cluster)

hz <- read_csv("../../minerva/Hazards/Datasets/DT-hzcats-society-level.csv")
IA_vars <- c(
  "ID",
  "allhazards_est_count_30_IA",
  "disaster_est_count_30_IA",
  "normative_est_count_30_IA",
  "allhazards_H9a_sev_exposure_30_IA",
  "allhazards_sev_exposure_30_IA"
)
# Keep IDs + variables before scaling
IA_for_clust <- hz %>%
  select(ID, all_of(IA_vars)) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(IA_vars))


IA_scaled <- hz %>%
  select(all_of(IA_vars)) %>%
  drop_na() %>%
  select(-ID) %>% 
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ as.numeric(scale(.))))


```

```{r}
fviz_nbclust(IA_scaled, kmeans, method = "wss")

kmeans_pre <- kmeans(
  IA_scaled,
  centers = 4,
  nstart = 20
)

print(kmeans_pre)

fviz_cluster(kmeans_pre, data = IA_scaled)

# Table with IDs, original values, and 4-cluster assignment
cluster_table_pre <- IA_for_clust %>%
  mutate(cluster_4 = kmeans_pre$cluster) %>%
  arrange(cluster_4, ID)

View(cluster_table_pre)
```

```{r}
# 2 cases look like outliers
# Keep the cluster labels attached so IDs stay aligned
outliers <- cluster_table_pre %>%
  filter(cluster_4 == 3)

IA_scaled_with_clusters <- IA_scaled %>%
  mutate(cluster_4 = kmeans_pre$cluster)

hz_no_outliers <- IA_scaled_with_clusters %>%
  filter(cluster_4 != 3) %>%
  select(-cluster_4)

# Keep the matching original ID/value rows, but remove the old cluster label
IA_for_clust_no_outliers <- IA_for_clust %>%
  mutate(cluster = kmeans_pre$cluster) %>%
  filter(cluster != 3) %>%
  select(-cluster)

fviz_nbclust(hz_no_outliers, kmeans, method = "wss")

kmeans_no <- kmeans(
  hz_no_outliers,
  centers = 3,
  nstart = 20
)

print(kmeans_no)

fviz_cluster(kmeans_no, data = hz_no_outliers)

# Final table with IDs, original values, and ONLY the no-outlier cluster category
cluster_table_no_outliers <- IA_for_clust_no_outliers %>%
  mutate(cluster = kmeans_no$cluster) %>%
  arrange(cluster, ID)

View(cluster_table_no_outliers)
write.csv(cluster_table_no_outliers, "results/cluster_cats_no_outliers.csv")
```


# boxplots

```{r}
hazard_vars <- c(
  "allhazards_est_count_30_IA",
  "disaster_est_count_30_IA",
  "normative_est_count_30_IA",
  "allhazards_H9a_sev_exposure_30_IA",
  "allhazards_sev_exposure_30_IA"
)

cluster_boxplot_data <- cluster_table_no_outliers %>%
  mutate(cluster = factor(cluster)) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(hazard_vars),
    names_to = "hazard_variable",
    values_to = "value"
  )

ggplot(cluster_boxplot_data, aes(x = cluster, y = value, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = cluster),
    width = 0.15,
    alpha = 0.5,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ hazard_variable, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Hazard variables",
    title = "Distribution of Hazard Variables by Cluster Category",
    fill = "Cluster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
